cards.front: What is the main thread responsible for?
cards.back: The main thread is responsible for handling the startup of the program and executing the main method.

cards.front: What is the name of the thread class in Java?
cards.back: The Thread class in Java represents a thread of execution.

cards.front: What is the difference between a thread and a process?
cards.back: A thread is a lightweight process that shares the same memory space as other threads in the same process.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the Thread.start() method?
cards.back: The Thread.start() method starts a new thread of execution.

cards.front: What is the main difference between a sleep and yield method?
cards.back: The sleep method blocks the current thread for a specified amount of time, while the yield method temporarily gives up the current thread but allows other threads to run.
